npcs.name: Augustus Larkin
npcs.agenda: Lead the expedition to the highland pyramid and break or strip the golden ward so the Father of Maggots can be freed—while insisting he is only a sick treasure-hunter.
npcs.fear: Being exposed as a vessel of Nyarlathotep before the ward is broken; losing de Mendoza's protection.
npcs.secret: M'Weru drugged him and tattooed the Bloody Tongue on his chest; the Mask wears him increasingly as the chapter progresses.
npcs.voice: Colonial charm fraying into feverish insistence. Talks treasure, press coverage, and 'nothing to worry about.'
npcs.keeper_note: Classic: mostly human until late. Pulp: possession can surge. Stats in Characters and Monsters: Peru.

npcs.name: Luis de Mendoza
npcs.agenda: Guard Larkin, shepherd the party to the pyramid, and feed the Father of Maggots—eliminating threats (including Nayra) as needed.
npcs.fear: Permanent severance from the imprisoned god if the ward is fully restored.
npcs.secret: He is an intelligent kharisiri (undead conquistador), not a mortal bodyguard.
npcs.voice: Sparse Spanish-accented English. Polite, cold, watchful.
npcs.keeper_note: In Lima he usually stays human-seeming; open horror reserved for highlands/ruins unless pulp pacing demands earlier reveals.

npcs.name: Nayra
npcs.agenda: Survive kharisiri attention, help trusted friends understand the pyramid threat, and refuse to march into the ruins herself.
npcs.fear: Leading hunters to her reed-island refuge; dying before she can warn others.
npcs.secret: She knows enough folklore and geography to pinpoint the pyramid and identify de Mendoza as kharisiri.
npcs.voice: Warm, teasing Aymara elder; Elias translates. Defuses tension with jokes, then turns deadly serious about the sealed god.
npcs.keeper_note: Will heal injuries; resists joining the trek. Pulp: island ambush possible after the meeting.

npcs.name: Jackson Elias (a.k.a. Jesse Hughes)
npcs.agenda: Infiltrate Larkin's expedition, confirm the kharisiri cult, and gather leads that later connect to the Carlyle Expedition.
npcs.fear: Being unmasked to Larkin too early; dying before the wider story is told.
npcs.secret: Traveling under the Jesse Hughes alias; already consulted Nayra once and followed Larkin to Lima.
npcs.voice: Worldly journalist—curious, wry, suddenly intense when cult evidence appears.
npcs.keeper_note: Primary hook into the larger Masks campaign after Peru concludes.

npcs.name: Trinidad Rizo
npcs.agenda: Help document Larkin's destination for Professor Sánchez and impress as a capable undergraduate researcher.
npcs.fear: Being buried—literally—under storeroom artifacts or dismissed as a meddling student.
npcs.secret: Her notes already triangulate folklore and Larkin's press claims; she can be endangered in the university storeroom.
npcs.voice: Eager, rapid Spanish/English mix; academic excitement.
npcs.keeper_note: Optional Lima research face; storeroom mishap set piece on p.68.
